class.name: FmodAudioEffectRecord
class.brief_description: Records audio input or output to a WAV file.
class.description: FmodAudioEffectRecord captures audio data passing through an audio bus and records it to an internal buffer. When recording is complete, the captured audio can be saved as an [AudioStreamWAV] resource. This effect is useful for recording gameplay audio, capturing microphone input (when applied to a microphone bus), or creating audio samples from synthesized sounds. To start recording, apply the effect to an audio bus and set [member recording_active] to [code]true[/code]. To stop, set [member recording_active] to [code]false[/code] and call [method get_recording] to retrieve the recorded audio as an [AudioStreamWAV].

method.name: get_recording
method.description: Returns the recorded audio as an [AudioStreamWAV] resource. The returned audio stream can be played back, saved to disk, or used like any other audio resource. This method should be called after setting [member recording_active] to [code]false[/code] to stop the recording.

members.member: The audio format used for recording. See [enum Format] for available options. The default [constant FORMAT_16_BITS] provides good quality and reasonable file size.

If [code]true[/code], the effect is currently recording audio from the bus. Set to [code]true[/code] to start recording and [code]false[/code] to stop. After stopping, call [method get_recording] to retrieve the captured audio.

constants.constant: 8-bit PCM audio format. Smaller file size but lower audio quality with audible quantization noise. Suitable for low-quality recordings or when memory is constrained.
16-bit PCM audio format (default). Good balance between audio quality and file size. Recommended for most recording applications.
IMA ADPCM compressed audio format. Significantly smaller file size with acceptable quality. Useful when storage space is limited, though slightly lower quality than PCM formats.
